edge: Social media posts provide a basis for personality detection
Early researchers used psychological lexicons like LIWC and MRC to analyze word usage patterns for extracting personality traits from text.
LIWC is a tool for identifying statistical patterns in language that relate to personality traits.
MRC lexicon was another source used by early researchers to analyze language and connect it to personality traits.
Social media data supports various downstream applications such as human-computer interaction
The effectiveness of personality detection contributes to the development and improvement of these applications.

Social media users generate large amounts of content that reveals their psychological activities.

Psychological vocabulary-based models use 89 features extracted by the LIWC API to predict personality traits.
These LIWC features are fed into an SVM model for personality trait predictions.
The supervised detection process minimizes the MSE loss
The MSE loss is used to minimize the difference between the predicted personality score and the actual personality traits for a given data point.
